LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS 10 AND 19 TO THE O'TOOLE DECLARATION. EVEN IF O'TOOLE IS THE CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS, THE DECLARATION FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THE "PRAYER FOR DAMAGES WORKSHEETS" THAT ARE ATTACHED TO THE O'TOOLE DECLARATION AS EXHIBITS F AND J, AND WHICH ARE THE BASIS FOR HER ASSERTION THAT $207,302.20 IS OWED, ARE BUSINESS RECORDS UNDER EVIDENCE CODE 1270 AND 1271. THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED AND SERVED. THE MOTION DOES NOT VIOLATE CCP SECTION 1008, SINCE THE FIRST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LYON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The Court sustains defendant's evidentiary objections 10 and 19 to the O'Toole declaration. Even if O'Toole is the custodian of records, the declaration fails to establish that the "prayer for damages worksheets" that are attached to the O'Toole declaration as Exhibits F and J, and which are the basis for her assertion that $207,302.20 is owed, are business records under Evidence Code 1270 and 1271. The motion was timely filed and served. The motion does not violate CCP section 1008, since the first motion for summary judgment was denied without prejudice. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
